inputs.name: q
inputs.desc: Query tensor [num_heads, num_tokens, head_dim] to be normalized in-place

inputs.name: k
inputs.desc: Key tensor [num_kv_heads, num_tokens, head_dim] to be normalized in-place

weights.name: q_gamma
weights.desc: Q norm gamma weights [head_dim], shared across all heads

weights.name: k_gamma
weights.desc: K norm gamma weights [head_dim], shared across all heads

strategies.name: head
strategies.notes: Each head is independent. Parallelize across heads for Q, then across kv_heads for K.

strategies.name: token
strategies.notes: Parallelize across tokens; each thread handles one or more tokens across all heads.

constraints.notes: D (head_dim) should be multiple of 64 for optimal SIMD in underlying rmsnorm_forward.

impl.c_declaration: void qk_norm_forward(float *q, float *k, const float *q_gamma, const float *k_gamma, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, float eps);

parity.notes: Verify per-head RMSNorm matches PyTorch torch.nn.RMSNorm applied per-head

notes: Per-head RMSNorm on Q and K (Qwen3-style). Wraps rmsnorm_forward twice: once for Q with num_heads*num_tokens rows, once for K with num_kv_heads*num_tokens rows. Each row is [head_dim] elements. Gamma is shared across all heads (one gamma vector per Q, one per K). Placed between QKV projection and RoPE to stabilize attention scores.
